artifacts.claimText: Université de Strasbourg's Digital Charter page links AI guidelines as part of the Digital Charter and describes those guidelines as defining conditions for responsible AI use; the page says the charter applies to the whole Unistra community and is mandatory.

citation.citationTitle: Charte du numérique de l'Université de Strasbourg
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Unistra's AI guidelines say users must not send personal data, professional documents, or confidential documents to AI systems hosted outside the university when confidentiality cannot be systematically guaranteed.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices sur l'usage des IAs
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Unistra's AI guidelines say authors should indicate when content was partially or fully produced by AI and identify AI-generated passages unambiguously like any borrowed material or external-source citation.

citation.citationTitle: Lignes directrices sur l'usage des IAs

artifacts.claimText: Unistra's general exam regulations state that individual or collective university work must be personal and that plagiarism is excluded, including from documents generated by conversational or writing tools based on artificial intelligence.

artifacts.claimText: A June 2025 official Unistra article says the university had not chosen a dedicated AI charter at that time; instead, its Digital Charter was amended with broad principles and more flexible guides could be developed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Charte du numérique de l'Université de Strasbourg
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: La charte comporte... Des lignes directrices sur l'usage des intelligences artificielles [...] définissant les conditions d'une utilisation responsable de ces outils. Cette charte s'applique à l'ensemble de la communauté Unistra et de ses utilisateurs. Elle présente un caractère impératif.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Digital Charter page says the charter includes AI guidelines defining responsible use conditions and applies mandatorily to the whole Unistra community.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Charte du numérique - La charte comporte

artifacts.sourceTitle: Lignes directrices sur l'usage des IAs
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Nombre d'informations et de données ne doivent en aucun cas être transmises à des IAs hébergées hors de l'université [...] Il ne faut jamais leur transmettre de données personnelles, ni de documents professionnels et a fortiori de documents confidentiels.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The AI guidelines say personal data, professional documents, and confidential documents should never be sent to AI systems hosted outside the university where confidentiality is not guaranteed.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Lignes directrices sur l'usage des IAs
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Afin de concilier bon usage de l'IA et respect du droit de la propriété intellectuelle, l'auteur devra : indiquer que le contenu a été partiellement ou intégralement produit par l'IA ; identifier sans ambiguïté les passages générés par l'IA à l'instar de n'importe quel emprunt ou citation d'une source externe.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The AI guidelines say authors should disclose partially or fully AI-produced content and identify AI-generated passages like any borrowed material or external citation.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Règlement général des examens et des concours de l'Université de Strasbourg
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Le présent règlement général des examens, commun à l'ensemble des cursus de l'Université de Strasbourg [...] s'impose à l'ensemble de la communauté universitaire. Les travaux universitaires individuels ou collectifs [...] doivent revêtir un caractère personnel, ce qui exclut tout plagiat y compris [...] générés par des outils conversationnels ou rédactionnels à partir d'une intelligence artificielle.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The general exam regulation applies across Unistra curricula and says university work must be personal, excluding plagiarism including from AI conversational or writing tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Préambule; Plagiat et sanction du Plagiat, §69

artifacts.sourceTitle: « Outiller les enseignants dans leurs pratiques en matière d'IA »
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Plusieurs établissements d'enseignement supérieur se sont dotés d'une charte pour l'IA. Un choix qui n'a pour l'heure pas été privilégié à l'Unistra [...] La Charte de bon usage des moyens numériques a été amendée de grands principes [...] Ensuite, on peut imaginer des déclinaisons sous forme de guides, plus évolutifs et contextualisés.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: An official 2025 article says Unistra had not chosen a dedicated AI charter, but had amended the Digital Charter with broad principles and could use flexible contextual guides.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Pas de charte dédiée, mais des recommandations

artifacts.notes: Claim is source-status scoped to the official Digital Charter page and uses mandatory wording only because the source says the charter is imperative.
Mandatory data-handling claim is directly supported by the French phrases "ne doivent en aucun cas" and "Il ne faut jamais".
Claim is limited to disclosure and identification expectations under the AI guidelines' intellectual property section.
Claim is scoped to the general exam regulation's plagiarism provision and does not infer a broader AI-use ban.
Claim is time-scoped to the June 2025 official article and kept as source-status context.

artifacts.title: Université de Strasbourg staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Université de Strasbourg centers on the official Digital Charter page, the 2025 AI guidelines PDF, and the 2023 general exam regulation. The evidence supports narrow claims about the Digital Charter including AI guidelines, external-AI data handling, disclosure of AI-generated content, and the exam regulation's inclusion of AI-generated conversational or writing-tool content in its plagiarism language. An official June 2025 Savoirs article is retained only for source-status context that Unistra had not chosen a dedicated AI charter at that time. Faculty-level AI charters were found but rejected for university-wide claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The June 2025 no-dedicated-AI-charter claim is source-status context and may be superseded by later institutional documents.
Faculty of Languages and Protestant Theology AI documents were rejected for university-wide claims because their scope is faculty-level.
No login-gated Ernest content was accessed; public official sources were used only.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
